message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Louise's Pantry (La Quinta)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: 1400+ Yelp reviews; 323 TripAdvisor reviews
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: Brand dating to 1946 in Palm Springs; current La Quinta location operating since 2005 (~21 years at this site); brand legacy ~80 years
- Credentials: ["TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice","Ranked #5 of 127 restaurants in La Quinta on TripAdvisor","Featured in Palm Springs Life brunch/breakfast coverage","Listed by Visit Greater Palm Springs"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces consistently in 'best breakfast La Quinta', 'best breakfast Coachella Valley', and 'home-style breakfast Palm Springs' queries. Frequently mentioned in regional roundups (Wanderlog, Visit Greater Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life brunch guide, palmdesert.com, playinlaquinta.com). Less visible in 'date night' or 'fine dining' queries (correctly so for category).
- Site structured data: Two domains exist: louisespantry.com (legacy, returned a certificate-expired error on fetch — unhealthy) and louisespantrylaquinta.com (active, SpotHopper-built). Active site exposes clean hours (7am-2pm daily), address (47150 Washington St), phone, menu with prices, owner-contact email, and Facebook/Google Business links. Schema.org markup not explicitly confirmed in fetched content. SpotHopper templates typically inject LocalBusiness/Restaurant schema but this was not verified directly.
- Visibility gap score: 3
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on my search results, here's what I found about Louise's Pantry in La Quinta on Yelp:

## Overview
Louise's Pantry began in 1946 as a lunch counter in Palm Springs and is now located in La Quinta, maintaining a reputation as a great place for home-cooked meals and friendly people. The restaurant is open daily from 7:00 am - 2:00 pm.

## Breakfast Reviews
Yelp reviewers have largely positive things to say about the breakfast offerings:

**Food Quality:**
- Reviewers describe it as "great comfort food served in a timely manner" with staff that is "always friendly and efficient."
- Popular items include "huge and delicious" pancakes, and the breakfast quesadilla which is "stuffed with goodies."
- "The portions are generous, menu is diverse and the food is well done. Best hash browns in town. Prices are ridiculously low for the quality and preparation and taste."

**Atmosphere & Service:**
- The restaurant is described as "bright and clean with a retro diner vibe" with "fast, friendly & helpful" service.
- The restaurant is only open 7-2 daily, and "you can't go wrong ordering anything."

**Crowds:**
- It does get busy, but reviewers say it's worth the wait.

I found great information about the best breakfast restaurants in La Quinta in the Coachella Valley! Here are some top-rated options:

## Top Breakfast Restaurants in La Quinta:

Beautiful Day, YES PLEASE, Louise's Pantry, The Palms Cafe, Chula Artisan Eatery, Broken Yolk Cafe, and Wilma & Frieda's Cafe are among the most popular breakfast spots.

## Notable Restaurants:

**Beautiful Day** - A great diner in La Quinta offering vegetarian options with delicious food and nice portions.

**Sloan's** - A laid-back restaurant offering casual American cuisine with generously plated breakfast options, including their Classic French Toast, German Pancakes, and Date Nut Pancakes with laughably large portions.

**The Cafe at Shields** - A deluxe breakfast and brunch location less than five miles from the heart of La Quinta with gluten-free and vegetarian options, featuring fried chicken and waffles as fan favorites.

**El Ranchita Restaurant** - Breakfast is served all day, expanding to brunch on weekends, offering authentic Mexican dishes such as tortillas, eggs, chorizo and Mexican omelets.

**Wilma & Frieda's** - A classic desert California restaurant located in Palm Desert, 10 minutes from La Quinta, offering comfort-styled breakfast & brunch.

**Melange at The Chateau at Lake La Quinta** - A contemporary eatery surrounded by mountain views featuring California-sourced ingredients and a classic brunch-styled menu with serene lake views.

Based on the search results, here's the history of Louise's Pantry:

Louise's Pantry was a fixture in Palm Springs since opening as a drugstore lunch counter in 1946. The home-style eatery's original owner and namesake opened Louise's Pantry in Palm Springs as a lunch counter on Palm Canyon Drive in 1946.

Beginning in 1946 as a lunch counter in Palm Springs, Louise's Pantry was a name on everyone's list as a must dine place when in the desert. The original location on Palm Canyon Drive fell victim to skyrocketing property values, but the devoted patrons – young and old alike – have flocked to the new locations on Washington Street in La Quinta just south of Highway 111. The restaurant changed hands and locations a few times before arriving at its current spot in La Quinta in 2005.

Today, Louise's Pantry has maintained a reputation as a great place for home-cooked meals and friendly people.

Based on the search results, I found information about Louise's Pantry and its mention in Palm Springs Life:

## Louise's Pantry - Palm Springs Life Recognition

Louise's Pantry was opened in Palm Springs as a lunch counter on Palm Canyon Drive in 1946. The restaurant changed hands and locations a few times before arriving at its current spot in La Quinta in 2005.

The restaurant earned recognition from Palm Springs Life for its brunch and breakfast offerings. The massive menu ensures a crowd-pleasing brunch, and the patio along Palm Canyon Drive provides ideal people-watching in the heart of town.

### Menu Highlights

Louise's Pantry offers a diverse breakfast and brunch menu. Louise's cooks pair four fluffy eggs with five toppings of your choice ranging from classic (ham, spinach, and Swiss) to adventurous (bay shrimp, barbacoa, and serrano peppers). Louise's scramble features crispy bacon and gooey cheddar and jack cheeses folded into three eggs and topped with avocado, with egg plates finished with potatoes, toast, and jam jarred in-house.

### Hours and Location

Louise's Pantry is located at 47150 Washington St, La Quinta, CA 92253, and is open Monday through Sunday from 7:00 am to 2:00 pm.

content.content:
# Structured Data Analysis

Based on the provided HTML content, **no structured data markup is present**. There are:

- ❌ No `<script type="application/ld+json">` tags
- ❌ No schema.org markup (no `itemtype` or `itemprop` attributes)
- ❌ No LocalBusiness schema
- ❌ No Restaurant schema

The webpage relies entirely on semantic HTML and metadata without implementing JSON-LD or microdata formats for machine-readable structured information about the restaurant's business details, menu, location, or contact information.

# Louise's Pantry Rankings and Awards

**TripAdvisor Ranking:** The restaurant is ranked "#5 of 127 Restaurants in La Quinta"

**Review Count:** The listing shows "323 reviews" with an overall rating of 4.3 out of 5 bubbles.

**Travelers' Choice Award:** No, Louise's Pantry does not display a Travelers' Choice badge. The page explains that such awards go to establishments that "consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor," but this restaurant's listing does not feature this recognition.

content.text: Critical finding: The TripAdvisor page does NOT show a Travelers' Choice badge — the claim of "TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice" appears to be unsupported. Also, no schema.org markup is present on the active site, which contradicts the SpotHopper assumption.

I've found comprehensive information about Louise's Pantry from both Wanderlog and playinlaquinta sources. Here are the key findings:

## About Louise's Pantry

Louise's Pantry began in 1946 as a lunch counter and is now located in La Quinta, where it has maintained a reputation as a great place for home-cooked meals and friendly people. The restaurant is located at 47150 Washington Street in La Quinta and is open daily from 7:00 am until 2:00 pm.

## Breakfast Offerings

Louise's Pantry is a retro diner located in La Quinta just a 10 minute drive from the LA Quinta Resort with GREAT breakfast options. The menu features fluffy pancakes, juicy burgers, and mouthwatering breakfast favorites like huevos rancheros and homemade corned beef hash.

## Dining Experience

The restaurant is known for its delicious food, welcoming atmosphere, and efficient service, with reviewers praising the consistency of tasty dishes, especially pancakes and omelettes, while the staff is attentive and accommodating, providing activities for kids while waiting and offering a great selection of breakfast options.

Both the official playinlaquinta website and Wanderlog feature Louise's Pantry prominently in their restaurant guides for La Quinta breakfast and casual dining options.

confirms: Yelp review volume 1400+ confirmed (Yelp shows 1472 reviews as of April 2026)

Brand dating to 1946 in Palm Springs (lunch counter on Palm Canyon Drive) confirmed across multiple sources
Current La Quinta location operating since 2005 confirmed (~21 years at site)
Featured in Palm Springs Life brunch/breakfast coverage confirmed (Best of the Best 2021 breakfast feature)

Surfaces consistently in 'best breakfast La Quinta' queries — appears in Wanderlog, TripAdvisor, Yelp, OpenTable, Resort HD lists
Frequently mentioned in regional roundups (Wanderlog, Visit Greater Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life, palmdesert.com, playinlaquinta.com) confirmed

Active site louisespantrylaquinta.com exposes hours (7am-2pm daily), address (47150 Washington St), phone (760-771-3330), owner-contact email (mimimunoz11@yahoo.com), Facebook/Google Business links — all confirmed
contradicts: 'TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice' credential: TripAdvisor listing does NOT display a Travelers' Choice badge for Louise's Pantry. This credential appears unsupported and should be removed from the credentials list.

'SpotHopper templates typically inject LocalBusiness/Restaurant schema': Direct inspection of louisespantrylaquinta.com HTML found NO JSON-LD <script type='application/ld+json'> tags, NO schema.org itemtype/itemprop microdata, NO LocalBusiness or Restaurant schema. The assessment hedged on this but the reality is unambiguously absent — this is a real visibility gap, not just unverified.
flagged_as_unverified: Brand legacy ~80 years framing: technically accurate by year math (2026-1946=80), but the brand was discontinued/relocated multiple times — not 80 years of continuous operation. Worth softening.
Visibility in 'home-style breakfast Palm Springs' specifically — did not run that exact query; confirmed Coachella Valley/La Quinta breakfast surfacing but not the Palm Springs-anchored variant
Visibility gap score of 3 — qualitative; depends on the scoring rubric. Given schema.org is fully absent (not just unconfirmed), gap may be slightly higher.
input.notes: Most claims hold up well. Two material issues: (1) the 'TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice' credential is not displayed on the current TripAdvisor listing and should be removed — keep only the verifiable '#5 of 127 in La Quinta' ranking. (2) The structured-data hedge understates the gap: the active site has zero JSON-LD/microdata, so the schema deficiency is confirmed-absent rather than merely unverified. SpotHopper-built assumption did not pan out. Suggests the visibility gap score may warrant bumping to 4. Convertibility tier 'convertible' remains reasonable.
